subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Explain how the British collected land revenue in India
Understand the features of the Permanent Settlement
Analyse the role of zamindars, peasants and moneylenders
Understand the system of indigo cultivation
Examine the problems faced by indigo farmers
Explain the causes and outcomes of the Indigo Revolt

lesson_plan.title: British Land Revenue Policies
topics_covered: Importance of land revenue for the British administration
Early British attempts to collect revenue
student_activities: Discuss how farmers produce crops and pay taxes
List reasons why the British depended on land revenue

lesson_plan.title: Permanent Settlement System
student_activities: Write the features of the Permanent Settlement

lesson_plan.title: Zamindars, Peasants and Rural Society
student_activities: Discuss problems faced by peasants due to high taxes

lesson_plan.title: Indigo Cultivation System
topics_covered: Demand for indigo dye in Europe
The advance system used by planters
student_activities: Explain how indigo was grown by peasants
Discuss why indigo cultivation was not profitable for farmers

lesson_plan.title: Problems of Indigo Farmers
student_activities: Write two problems faced by indigo farmers
Discuss how farmers tried to resist the system

lesson_plan.title: Indigo Revolt and Chapter Recap
topics_covered: Outcomes and importance of the revolt
student_activities: Write the causes of the Indigo Revolt
Participate in a group quiz for revision

teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect the land revenue system with the everyday life of farmers to help them better understand the social and economic impact of British policies.
